text: Step outside for a 10-minute walk around the block.
Do a 5-minute stretching routine — try hip circles, neck rolls, and a forward fold.
Go for a 20-minute jog or brisk walk in your neighbourhood.
Do 3 sets of bodyweight exercises: push-ups, squats, and lunges.
Grab your bike and ride to a destination you'd normally drive to.
Spend 15 minutes drawing or doodling whatever comes to mind.
Write 3 pages in a journal about anything you're feeling right now.
Pick up an instrument and play for 20 minutes — no pressure to be perfect.
Write a short poem or haiku about something you noticed today.
Try your hand at origami — fold a paper crane or a simple boat.
Call or text a friend you haven't spoken to in a while.
Plan a meal or outing with someone you care about for this week.
Write a handwritten note or card to someone who made a difference in your life.
Invite a neighbour or colleague for a short walk or coffee.
Play a board game or card game with whoever is nearby.
Sit comfortably and try a 5-minute breathing exercise: 4 counts in, 4 hold, 4 out.
Spend 10 minutes in a body scan meditation — notice sensations from head to toe.
Write down 3 things you're genuinely grateful for right now.
Step outside and observe 5 things you can see, 4 you can touch, 3 you can hear.
Brew a cup of tea mindfully — smell it, feel the warmth, sip slowly.
Read a physical book for 20 minutes — no bookmarks on your phone.
Pick up a magazine or newspaper and read one full article without skimming.
Learn 5 new words in a language you've always wanted to speak.
Watch a documentary on a topic outside your usual interests.
Sit in a park or garden and observe the natural world around you for 15 minutes.
Water your plants — or start a small herb garden on your windowsill.
Watch the sunrise or sunset for at least 5 minutes without your phone.
Cook a meal from scratch — no meal kit, no shortcuts.
Try baking something simple: banana bread, muffins, or cookies.
Prep your meals for tomorrow — chop veggies, cook grains, pack snacks.
Put on an album you love and listen from start to finish without multitasking.
Sing along to your favourite songs for 10 minutes — alone is fine!
Go for a late-night walk under the stars — bring a jacket and leave your phone at home.
Do a 10-minute yoga flow — child's pose, cat-cow, downward dog.
Challenge a friend to a physical game: frisbee, basketball, or a race.
